components.desc: Data layer: JSON file persistence (stdlib json) with functions add_habit(name), mark_done(name, date), load/save. Habits stored as {name: [iso-date strings]}. Reject duplicate names, duplicate same-day marks. Self-test: script adds habits, marks dates, reloads file, asserts state.

Pure functions: current_streak(dates, today) and best_streak(dates) computing consecutive-day runs from list of iso dates. Handles gaps, today-not-yet-marked (streak alive if yesterday marked). Self-test: asserts on fixed date fixtures.

render(habits, today) -> str producing plain-ANSI table: habit name, done-today marker, current/best streak. Also CLI entry `--render` that loads store, prints table, exits 0 (non-interactive test mode). Self-test: render fixture habits, assert expected substrings in output.

argparse CLI: `add NAME`, `done NAME` (marks today), `--render`. Wires store + view. Errors (unknown habit, dup add) exit nonzero with message. Self-test: subprocess invocations against temp data file, assert exit codes and output.

Interactive curses screen: list habits with streaks, keys j/k navigate, space toggles done-today, a adds habit (prompt), q quits. Reuses store + streak logic; rendering logic shared with render-view where practical. Self-test: non-interactive — import module, verify key-handler functions mutate state correctly without curses init (logic separated from screen I/O).
